verdict_reason: Search wiring and AC2 messaging are correct, but the 400-swallowing fallback both hides genuine backend errors and is unreachable against the current backend contract

todos.issue: Any backend 400 is converted into a 200 'No notifications found' page whenever referenceNumber is set, so a genuine bad request (malformed sort, out-of-range page, contract drift) is silently presented to the trader as an empty search result
todos.fix: Narrow the branch — check the ProblemDetail type/field before swallowing, and log at warn level so the swallowed 400 is still observable

todos.issue: The 400 fallback is unreachable against the current backend — NotificationController accepts referenceNumber with no @Pattern and NotificationIT asserts free text returns 200 with an empty page, so no 400 is ever produced for a search
todos.fix: Either add the @Pattern constraint backend-side so the branch is real, or drop the branch and rely on the empty-page response

todos.issue: renderEmptySearchResult repeats the full happy-path view model (pageTitle, heading, sortOptions, listQuerySuffix) rather than sharing it, so the two render paths can drift — it already omits notifications-count fields the catch-all path sets
todos.fix: Extract a buildHomeViewModel({ notifications, resultsLabel, pagination, ... }) helper used by all three h.view calls

todos.issue: The conditional spread ...(referenceNumber ? { referenceNumber } : {}) is redundant — notificationClient.findAll already guards with if (referenceNumber) before setting the search param, and parseReferenceNumber already returns undefined for blank input
